developer_goal: Start a JFrog Platform Cloud trial, configure a Docker repository, and push hello-world.

first_success.outcome: docker push succeeds and hello-world appears in the Cloud Docker repository Artifacts tree.
first_success.observable_signal: Artifactory Artifacts view shows the pushed hello-world image under the Docker repository.

route_selection.surface: JFrog Platform Cloud trial Docker hello-world push
route_selection.rule: Measure documented developer onboarding: account creation through first success. Prefer the vendor quickstart or hosted API/console path when one exists.
route_selection.selected: JFrog Platform Cloud trial Docker hello-world push

candidates.name: JFrog Platform Cloud trial Docker hello-world push
candidates.reason: JFrog Cloud trial onboarding creates a hosted Artifactory environment. Official Docker docs and Cloud testing guidance walk through repository setup, docker login to *.jfrog.io, and docker push of hello-world as the first artifact success.

required_path.action: Sign up for the JFrog Platform Cloud trial and log in with the emailed environment URL.
Create a Docker repository (local or virtual with default deployment repo) in Repositories.
required_path.observable_result: Repository appears in the repository list.

required_path.action: Run docker login SERVER_NAME.jfrog.io with Cloud credentials.
Pull/tag hello-world for SERVER_NAME.jfrog.io/REPO/hello-world:TAG and docker push.
Open Artifacts and confirm hello-world under the Docker repository.
required_path.observable_result: Image metadata visible in Artifacts tree.

prerequisites.description: JFrog Platform Cloud trial environment (URL, username, password from signup email).

uncertainties.question: Are all signup and first-success fields for JFrog fully enumerated in public docs?
uncertainties.impact: Atomic field counts inside account or billing steps may be incomplete.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Live console fields can differ from documentation screenshots.
